questions.question: What were some of the problems facing Akbar when he ascended the throne?
questions.answer: Akbar was only 13 years old. The Mughal throne was unstable and insecure, threatened by enemies on all sides: Adil Shah (nephew of Sher Shah) was determined to capture Delhi; the Rajputs had become powerful and were waiting to expel the Mughals; Kabul was occupied by Akbar's half-brother ruling independently.

questions.question: What is the significance of the Second Battle of Panipat?
questions.answer: In November 1556 CE, the Mughal army under Bairam Khan defeated Hemu (chief minister of Adil Shah) on the historic battlefield of Panipat. This crushed Afghan power and removed the threat to Akbar's throne, securing Mughal rule in India.

questions.question: What significant event happened when Akbar was 18 years old?
questions.answer: In 1560 CE, at the age of 18, Akbar decided to take charge of the affairs of the state himself. He dismissed Bairam Khan, his regent, and assumed absolute power and authority.

questions.question: Describe the Battle of Haldighati in 3-4 lines.
questions.answer: The Battle of Haldighati was fought in 1576 CE between Rana Pratap Singh and the Mughal forces led by Raja Man Singh of Amber. The Rajputs were outnumbered and defeated. Rana Pratap escaped to a mountain fortress and continued his efforts to recover his territories, but he could not recover Chittor.
key_points: Rana Pratap vs Raja Man Singh
Rana Pratap escaped and continued resistance

questions.question: Who was a 'Vakil' and what role did he play in Akbar's government?
questions.answer: The Vakil was the prime minister in Akbar's government. However, Akbar reduced the vakil's position to that of an advisory, ceremonial head. The vakil did not exercise any effective authority.

questions.question: Give two examples to show that Akbar had a policy of religious tolerance.
questions.answer: 1. Akbar abolished the jizya tax (imposed on non-Muslims) and the pilgrim tax (imposed on Hindus visiting places of pilgrimage). 2. He gave high posts to talented Hindus like Todar Mal, Birbal and Raja Bhagwan Das, and gave land grants to people of all religions.

questions.question: What is the Din-i-Ilahi?
questions.answer: Din-i-Ilahi (divine faith) was a new religious order announced by Akbar in 1582 CE. It was a combination of religious ideas of major religions, aimed at promoting universal brotherhood and national unity. Its main features were belief in one God and recognition of the emperor as God's representative on earth. It was more like a code of moral conduct with no sacred books, priestly classes, or rituals.

questions.question: Name any two famous monuments of Akbar.
questions.answer: Two famous monuments of Akbar are: 1. Fatehpur Sikri (his new capital city, referred to as a 'dream in stone', containing the Diwan-i-Khas, Panch Mahal, Jodha Bai's Palace and Buland Darwaza). 2. The Agra Fort, built in red sandstone.

questions.question: Adil Shah was the ___
questions.explanation: Adil Shah was the nephew of Sher Shah who was determined to capture the throne of Delhi and re-establish Afghan rule.

questions.question: Akbar married the daughter of ___
questions.explanation: Akbar married the daughter of the Raja of Jaipur (Amber) as part of his Rajput policy of matrimonial alliances.

questions.question: The principle of Sulh-i-Kul is ___
questions.explanation: Sulh-i-Kul means universal peace or peace with all. Akbar believed strongly in this principle.

questions.question: Many famous literary texts were translated into ___
questions.explanation: Akbar set up a translation department to translate Sanskrit works (Vedas, Mahabharata, Bhagavad Gita) and works from Arabic and Greek into Persian, the court language.
